title: Targeting Amazon Prime Day, Walmart Deals event moves to June
description: The retailer will give early deals access to Walmart+ members and is rolling out a campaign with a fictional boy band to spur interest in the sale.

title: Agency AI pitches are starting to face harder questions
description: As agencies race to sell proprietary AI the future of marketing, 3C Ventures argues advertisers need more proof.

title: AI ‘girlfriend ads’ are fueling a new wave of MFA sites
description: AI-generated “girlfriend ads” are driving traffic to made-for-advertising sites filled with low-quality content and ads.

title: Supporting Europe’s work in ensuring a trustworthy AI ecosystem
description: OpenAI supports the EU Code of Practice on AI content transparency, advancing provenance standards and tools to help people understand AI-generated content.

title: New Ecommerce Tools: June 10, 2026
description:
This week's rundown includes new services for livestream commerce, cryptocurrencies, visual search, logistics, AI-powered product intelligence, and more.
The post New Ecommerce Tools: June 10, 2026 appeared first on Practical Ecommerce.

title: Walmart, Wing add 7 markets to drone delivery expansion plan
description: The companies aim to start operations in Philadelphia, Phoenix and other metros by 2027 as the retailer advances its fast delivery push.

title: Adidas offers ecommerce-as-a-service (EaaS) featuring AI agents
description:
Apparel brand Adidas is running Audi Revolut F1 Team, offering ecommerce-as-a-service (shortened as EaaS or ECaaS) and integrating artificial intelligence (AI) agents from technology provider Salesforce. Adidas senior project manager Dominik Seeberger shared how the retailer leveraged Salesforce technology to launch EaaS, a new business model for Adidas. That unlocked a more than $100 million […]
The post Adidas offers ecommerce-as-a-service (EaaS) featuring AI agents appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: GrubMarket adds AI agent with goal to assist sales teams
description:
GrubMarket is adding a new artificial intelligence (AI)-powered tool to its portfolio of software agents aimed at food distributors. The new Sales AI Agent will focus on assisting sales teams in finding customers and generating price sheets, GrubMarket said in a June 8 press release. The San Francisco-based software-as-a-service (SAAS) company operates in the U.S., […]
The post GrubMarket adds AI agent with goal to assist sales teams appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: Here’s How to Use an AI Agent to Build a Cold Outreach Campaign
description: We had a campaign we wanted to get in front of the right people. The problem was familiar: We had a targeted list of business leaders we genuinely thought would benefit from what we were promoting, but no clean process for actually reaching them at scale. And we didn’t have enough time to do it the slow way [read: without AI].

title: FAQs for AEO: How to structure answers that rank in answer engines
description: AI search interfaces are reshaping how content gets surfaced and cited. Pew Research data from 2025 found that around one in five Google searches produced an AI-generated summary, with 88% of those summaries citing three or more sources. Bain’s 2025 research found that roughly 80% of consumers rely on zero-click results in at least 40% of their searches.

title: Accenture acquires Whalar agency to gain creator economy advantage
description: The shop has managed more than $600 million in creator campaigns and is credited with its creative and measurement expertise.

title: The problem for brands isn’t their marketing strategy — it’s their structure
description: Organizations that fail to deliver have often hired great talent into a broken system, writes Edmunds’ Alison Steinlauf Anziska.

title: How Jelly Belly is repositioning its brand for ‘social epicureans’
description: The new “Bean Appetit” campaign is meant to reintroduce the brand to consumers after its acquisition by confectioner Ferrara in 2023.

title: Marketing needs AI outcomes, not more AI pilots
description:
Learn how to identify high-value AI opportunities, prepare teams for adoption, and measure the outcomes that matter to the business.
The post Marketing needs AI outcomes, not more AI pilots appeared first on MarTech.

title: Your AI portfolio isn’t an AI strategy
description:
Learn the four questions that reveal whether your AI efforts are creating lasting value or simply generating activity.
The post Your AI portfolio isn’t an AI strategy appeared first on MarTech.
